food_name: Uttapam (South Indian Thick Vegetable Rice Pancake)
aliases: 印度厚蔬菜煎饼 (Yìndù hòu shūcài jiānbǐng)

chinese: 印度厚蔬菜煎饼 (Yìndù hòu shūcài jiānbǐng)

food_subgroup: South Indian thick fermented rice and urad dal pancake with vegetables; distinct from dosa (thin, crispy)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for uttapam specifically. Closest parent concept: Indian fermented rice and lentil pancake. FoodOn does not yet have a dedicated term for uttapam.
ontology.foodex2_description: South Indian thick fermented rice and lentil pancake with vegetables (uttapam); closest_parent match under fermented cereal-legume pancake category
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0SAG is used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 parent for fermented rice-lentil pancake products. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for uttapam.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for uttapam or South Indian fermented pancake dishes.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant-sized uttapam piece weighing approximately 200g, as served at South Indian restaurants such as Saravana Bhavan or Komala Vilas in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Uttapam — ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables (2017)
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research - National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN), India Food Composition Tables 2017
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Uttapam values sourced directly from ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables 2017, which includes fermented rice and urad dal pancake dishes. Values represent the cooked dish including griddle oil absorption and vegetable toppings as prepared in standard South Indian restaurant practice.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Fermented Rice and Lentil Pancake Products — analogous estimate based on idli/dosa batter GI data
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; peer-reviewed literature on fermented cereal-legume products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for uttapam exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 62 is estimated from the closest analogous fermented rice-lentil batter products (idli GI ~46–54; dosa GI ~67–77). Uttapam uses the same batter but is thicker and cooked with more oil, resulting in a moderately higher GI than idli but lower than plain white rice (GI ~72). GL per 200g serving = (62 × 19.0 × 2.0) / 100 = 23.6; input record states 21.7 using slightly different available carbohydrate basis. Value of 21.7 retained from source record as it reflects available carbohydrate after fibre adjustment. Fermentation lowers GI relative to unfermented rice products. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.guidance: Uttapam contains approximately 376mg sodium per standard 200g piece, which is within acceptable limits for hypertensive individuals; no added salt sauces are typically used, and the dish is naturally low in sodium compared to most restaurant meals, making it a suitable breakfast or snack choice for those managing blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Glycemic load ~22 per 200g piece (medium GI 62, medium GL)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Uttapam has a medium GI of approximately 62 and a glycemic load of ~22 per standard 200g piece; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume one piece as part of a balanced meal, pairing with sambar (lentil soup) or chutney to further moderate postprandial glucose response, and should avoid second servings to keep total GL within recommended limits.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.6g/100g), zero trans fat, vegan, griddle-cooked with vegetable oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Uttapam is low in saturated fat (1.2g per 200g piece) and contains zero trans fat and zero cholesterol, making it a heart-friendly South Indian breakfast option; the vegetable toppings (onion, tomato) contribute quercetin and lycopene with anti-inflammatory properties, and the dish is suitable for cardiovascular disease patients when prepared with minimal oil.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Low SFA, vegan, gluten-free, anti-inflammatory vegetable toppings
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Uttapam is vegan, gluten-free, and low in saturated fat; the onion and tomato toppings provide quercetin and lycopene respectively, which have documented anti-inflammatory properties relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Uttapam is a thick, soft South Indian pancake made from the same fermented rice and urad dal (black gram lentil) batter used for idli and dosa, but poured thicker (approximately 8–10mm) onto a hot griddle (tawa) with vegetable oil. Unlike the thin, crispy dosa, uttapam has a spongy, porous interior and a lightly crisped exterior. Diced onion, tomato, green chilli, and fresh coriander are pressed into the wet batter surface before cooking, creating a colourful, flavourful topping that becomes lightly charred and caramelised. Per 100g: 120 kcal, 3g protein, 4.5g fat (0.6g saturated), 19g carbohydrate, 1.5g sugar, 0.8g fibre, 188mg sodium. A standard restaurant piece weighs ~200g, providing ~240 kcal, 6g protein, 9g fat, 38g carbohydrate, and 376mg sodium. GI ~62 (medium), GL ~22 per piece (moderate). Uttapam is vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is a staple breakfast and tiffin item across Tamil Nadu and Karnataka, widely served at South Indian restaurants in Singapore including Saravana Bhavan and Komala Vilas. Compared to idli, uttapam is higher in fat and calories due to griddle oil absorption. Compared to dosa, it is thicker, softer, and lower in GI. Source: ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables 2017. Confidence: HIGH.

confidence_reason: Core nutritional values sourced directly from ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables 2017, which is the primary approved source for Indian dishes. GI is an analogous estimate from fermented rice-lentil batter products in the University of Sydney GI Database with confidence 0.9. All mandatory multilingual names are present. Ontology uses honest closest-parent matching with documented gap notes.
notes: Uttapam is a fermented rice and urad dal thick pancake; all core macros are non-zero and reflect the cooked dish including griddle oil absorption. Cholesterol is confirmed zero as this is a vegan dish with no animal-derived ingredients. Vitamin B12 is confirmed zero as uttapam is a plant-based fermented food with no animal products. Vitamin D is confirmed zero as no animal-derived or fortified ingredients are present. Trans fat is confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN source. Minor micronutrients (copper, manganese, vitamin K) sourced from enriched JSON with confidence 0.9. GI value of 62 is an analogous estimate; no direct uttapam GI measurement exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the processing date. FoodEx2 code A0SAG used as closest defensible parent; no exact code exists for uttapam. FoodOn URI not available for uttapam specifically.
